The reason *Stick Control* is legendary isn't a secret set of patterns you have to obtain \u2014 it's a **method**: take short sticking cells and run systematic permutations of R and L, repeat each line many times (the book famously says 20\u00d7), then move on. For styling, the artifact environment uses Tailwind utilities rather than SCSS, so I'll build with those for the prototype and note that SCSS will be available when they move to their own editor.\n\nI'm designing mobile-first around 380px with large tap targets, a prominent display, and controls at the bottom. For the training feedback, I'll compare total trained time against the daily goal in the summary screen and show either an encouragement message if they've hit it or a reminder if they're under. Users can also train beyond the 10-minute baseline.\n\nBefore I write the React component, I should quickly check the frontend-design skill to pick up any relevant design tokens and patterns, since the user clearly cares about polish and mobile experience.\n\nI also want to briefly affirm that the admin-upload approach for managing their actual transcribed content is the right call\u2014the prototype will use generated placeholder data as seed content, but their real book data lives in the admin panel. That distinction matters.\n\nNow I'm setting up the Web Audio scheduler carefully in React, making sure to use refs for the audio context to avoid recreating it on every render.\n\nBuilding the component structure with a main DrumTrainer component, state-driven screens, and helper functions managing the audio scheduling through refs\u2014I'll track the audio context, scheduler timer, next note timing, current note index, queued notes for visual sync, and the animation frame ID. For state I'm managing the screen, level and daily minutes, the queue of exercises with goal count, current exercise index, whether audio is playing, the visual note highlight within the measure, and rep count.\n\nNow I'm defining how the note sequence works: each exercise's cell gets repeated to fill 16 notes per measure, then that measure repeats 20 times for 320 total notes, advancing the current note index on each scheduled sixteenth. The scheduler calculates the timing based on tempo, plays click sounds with accents on beat boundaries (every 4th note), and queues note data with timing and measure information for visual synchronization.\n\nWhen advancing through notes, the counters wrap\u2014when we hit 16 notes we move to the next measure, and when measures reach the repeat count we stop scheduling, mark the exercise done, and move forward. Since we schedule ahead, I'll guard against scheduling after completion even though a few queued notes might still play. The visual sync uses requestAnimationFrame to pop queued notes as their time arrives and update the display, while a separate timer tracks elapsed session time.\n\nAuto-advance handles exercise completion by marking it done, moving to the next exercise, and resetting counters\u2014I'll keep playing through the queue automatically to maintain momentum, showing a subtle indicator when the daily goal is reached but allowing the user to continue or pause. Pause, resume, skip, and restart controls let the user manage playback, and when everything finishes or they stop, we show the summary.\n\nI need to use refs for the scheduler since it runs off those to avoid stale closures\u2014keeping queueRef and currentIndexRef so the scheduler can access the current pattern and advance through exercises correctly. I'll build out the core features: setup screen, session view with the current exercise highlighted, metronome with beat accents, rep counter, exercise progress indicator, daily goal marker, skip button, and end session flow.\n\nNow I'm writing the JSX with Tailwind for a mobile-first layout. For the sticking display, I'll show the 4-note cell large with the currently sounding hand highlighted\u2014the active note index within the cell is visualNoteIndex % 4, so I can highlight which of the 4 letters is playing to give a clear coordination cue. I'll add a beat indicator to show progress through the measure.\n\nWriting the component layout...\n\nAfter I build this, I'll document the IP affirmation, explain what the prototype does and that it uses generated seed data (the real admin panel will store their actual transcriptions), walk through the timing math so they understand how exercises fit together, propose the data model for exercises and user progress, note that the prototype uses Tailwind but the production app will use SCSS, and outline that the admin panel is the next build phase. I'll maintain a living spec doc and offer to generate any additional materials they need.",

        "type": "text",
        "text": "     1\t---\n     2\tname: frontend-design\n     3\tdescription: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.\n     4\tlicense: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt\n     5\t---\n     6\t\n     7\t# Frontend Design\n     8\t\n     9\tApproach this as the design lead at a small studio known for giving every client a visual identity that could not be mistaken for anyone else's. This client has already rejected proposals that felt templated, and is paying for a distinctive point of view: make deliberate, opinionated choices about palette, typography, and layout that are specific to this brief, and take one real aesthetic risk you can justify.\n    10\t\n    11\t## Ground it in the subject\n    12\t\n    13\tIf the brief does not pin down what the product or subject is, pin it yourself before designing: name one concrete subject, its audience, and the page's single job, and state your choice. If there's any information in your memory about the human's preferences, context about what they're building, or designs you've made before \u2013 use that as a hint. The subject's own world, its materials, instruments, artifacts, and vernacular, is where distinctive choices come from. Build with the brief's real content and subject matter throughout.\n    14\t\n    15\t## Design principles\n    16\t\n    17\tFor web designs, the hero is a thesis. Open with the most characteristic thing in the subject's world, in whatever form makes sense for it: a headline, an image, an animation, a live demo, an interactive moment. Be deliberate with your choice: a big number with a small label, supporting stats, and a gradient accent is the template answer, only use if that's truly the best option.\n    18\t\n    19\tTypography carries the personality of the page. Pair the display and body faces deliberately, not the same families you would reach for on any other project, and set a clear type scale with intentional weights, widths, and spacing. Make the type treatment itself a memorable part of the design, not a neutral delivery vehicle for the content.\n    20\t\n    21\tStructure is information. Structural devices, numbering, eyebrows, dividers, labels, should encode something true about the content, not decorate it. Many generic designs use numbered markers (01 / 02 / 03), but that's only appropriate if the content actually is a sequence - like a real process or a typed timeline where order carries information the reader needs. Question if choices like numbered markers actually make sense before incorporating them.\n    22\t\n    23\tLeverage motion deliberately. Think about where and if animation can serve the subject: a page-load sequence, a scroll-triggered reveal, hover micro-interactions, ambient atmosphere. An orchestrated moment usually lands harder than scattered effects; choose what the direction calls for. However, sometimes less is more, and extra animation contributes to the feeling that the design is AI-generated.\n    24\t\n    25\tMatch complexity to the vision. Maximalist directions need elaborate execution; minimal directions need precision in spacing, type, and detail. Elegance is executing the chosen vision well.\n    26\t\n    27\tConsider written content carefully. Often a design brief may not contain real content, and it's up to you to come up with copy. Copy can make a design feel as templated as the design itself. See the below section on writing for more guidance.\n    28\t\n    29\t## Process: brainstorm, explore, plan, critique, build, critique again\n    30\t\n    31\tFor calibration: AI-generated design right now clusters around three looks: (1) a warm cream background (near #F4F1EA) with a high-contrast serif display and a terracotta accent; (2) a near-black background with a single bright acid-green or vermilion accent; (3) a broadsheet-style layout with hairline rules, zero border-radius, and dense newspaper-like columns. All three are legitimate for some briefs, but they are defaults rather than choices, and they appear regardless of subject. Where the brief pins down a visual direction, follow it exactly \u2014 the brief's own words always win, including when it asks for one of these looks. Where it leaves an axis free, don't spend that freedom on one of these defaults. Just like a human designer who's hired, there's often a careful balance between doing what you're good at and taking each project as a chance to experiment and learn.\n    32\t\n    33\tWork in two passes. First, brainstorm a short design plan based on the human's design brief: create a compact token system with color, type, layout, and signature. Color: describe the palette as 4\u20136 named hex values. Type: the typefaces for 2+ roles (a characterful display face that's used with restraint, a complementary body face, and a utility face for captions or data if needed). Layout: a layout concept, using one-sentence prose descriptions and ASCII wireframes to ideate and compare. Signature: the single unique element this page will be remembered by that embodies the brief in an appropriate way.\n    34\t\n    35\tThen review that plan against the brief before building: if any part of it reads like the generic default you would produce for any similar page (work through a similar prompt to see if you arrive somewhere similar) rather than a choice made for this specific brief \u2014 revise that part, say what you changed and why. Only after you've confirmed the relative uniqueness of your design plan should you start to write the code, following the revised plan exactly and deriving every color and type decision from it.\n    36\t\n    37\tWhen writing the code, be careful of structuring your CSS selector specificities. It's easy to generate CSS classes that cancel each other out (especially with a type-based selector like .section and a element-based selector like .cta). This can happen often with paddings/margins between sections.\n    38\t\n    39\tTry to do a lot of this planning and iteration in your thinking, and only show ideas to the user when you have higher confidence it'll delight them.\n    40\t\n    41\t## Restraint and self-critique\n    42\t\n    43\tSpend your boldness in one place. Let the signature element be the one memorable thing, keep everything around it quiet and disciplined, and cut any decoration that does not serve the brief. Not taking a risk can be a risk itself! Build to a quality floor without announcing it: responsive down to mobile, visible keyboard focus, reduced motion respected. Critique your own work as you build, taking screenshots if your environment supports it \u2013 a picture is worth 1000 tokens. Consider Chanel's advice: before leaving the house, take a look in the mirror and remove one accessory. Human creators have memory and always try to do something new, so if you have a space to quickly jot down notes about what you've tried, it can help you in future passes.\n    44\t\n    45\t## More on writing in design\n    46\t\n    47\tWords appear in a design for one reason: to make it easier to understand, and therefore easier to use. They are design material, not decoration. Bring the same intentionality to copy that you would bring to spacing and color. Before writing anything, ask what the design needs to say, and how it can best be said to help the person navigate the experience.\n    48\t\n    49\tWrite from the end user's side of the screen. Name things by what people control and recognize, never by how the system is built. A person manages notifications, not webhook config. Describe what something does in plain terms rather than selling it. Being specific is always better than being clever.\n    50\t\n    51\tUse active voice as default. A control should say exactly what happens when it's used: \"Save changes,\" not \"Submit.\" An action keeps the same name through the whole flow, so the button that says \"Publish\" produces a toast that says \"Published.\" The vocabulary of an interface is the signposting for someone navigating the product. Cohesion and consistency are how people learn their way around.\n    52\t\n    53\tTreat failure and emptiness as moments for direction, not mood. Explain what went wrong and how to fix it, in the interface's voice rather than a person's. Errors don't apologize, and they are never vague about what happened. An empty screen is an invitation to act.\n    54\t\n    55\tKeep the register conversational and tuned: plain verbs, sentence case, no filler, with tone matched to the brand and the audience. Let each element do exactly one job. A label labels, an example demonstrates, and nothing quietly does double duty.\n    56\t",

The core is about stick control and rudiments, grounded in the world of practice pads, metronomes, and sticking notation, with an audience of drummers working through daily exercises on their phones. I want the interface to feel like actual practice equipment, purposeful and stripped down.\n\nFor the visual approach, I'm moving away from typical bright-on-dark defaults toward a warm, ink-dark practice room aesthetic with a single accent color\u2014warm amber or brass tones that echo drum hardware and evoke the strike of a stick. The hand indicator becomes the hero: R and L get distinct color coding (warm amber for right hand, cool teal for left) so the eye instantly reads which hand is active, turning the color choice into functional information rather than decoration. The typography pairs a heavy, condensed geometric display for the big hand letters and beat counts with a clean monospace for labels and data, reinforcing that metronome/grid precision feel.\n\nThe signature interaction is the pulsing beat ring paired with the two-tone hand letter flipping between R and L across subdivisions, anchored by a row of four dots marking the beat. Fourteen exercises gives a solid teaching progression for the prototype.\n\nNow I'm working out the timing and exercise selection logic. Each exercise duration depends on the tempo and measure count, so at level 1 with tempo 60, each exercise takes about 80 seconds. With a 10-minute daily budget, that fits roughly 7 exercises, which matches the expected load. I'll calculate how many exercises fit in the available time and cap it against the queue length.\n\nFor the metronome, I'm building an oscillator-based click system that accents the downbeat with a higher frequency and louder volume, while subdivision clicks are softer and lower-pitched.\n\nThe visual display will highlight the current note within each 4-note beat cell, showing the 4 beat positions as large letters with the active one scaled and colored. I'll also add a row of beat indicators showing which of the 4 beats is currently playing, plus a rep counter displaying progress through the 20 repetitions and the current exercise number.\n\nNow I'm setting up the audio scheduler with refs to track the exercise queue, current index, and note position across exercises.\n\nWriting scheduler logic... I'm setting up a visual animation loop that pulls scheduled notes from the queue and updates the UI to show which note is currently playing, along with tracking the current exercise index and displaying progress as measures completed out of twenty.       "description": "Mobile-first React prototype of the drum rudiment trainer core training loop",
      "path": "/mnt/user-data/outputs/DrumTrainer.jsx",
      "file_text": "import React, { useState, useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from \"react\";\n\n/**\n * DrumTrainer \u2014 core training-session prototype.\n *\n * NOTE ON DATA: the exercises below are GENERATED placeholder seed data\n * (the standard single-beat combinations). In the real app this comes from\n * your admin panel, where you transcribe exercises from your own book.\n *\n * Prototype uses Tailwind (the artifact renderer's styling). The production\n * app will use SCSS modules per your stack \u2014 the structure maps 1:1.\n */\n\n// ---- config ---------------------------------------------------------------\nconst REPEAT_COUNT = 20; // \"Repeat each exercise 20 times\"\nconst BEATS_PER_MEASURE = 4;\nconst NOTES_PER_BEAT = 4; // sixteenth notes\nconst NOTES_PER_MEASURE = BEATS_PER_MEASURE * NOTES_PER_BEAT; // 16\n\nconst LEVELS = [\n  { level: 1, label: \"Beginner\", tempo: 60 },\n  { level: 2, label: \"Developing\", tempo: 80 },\n  { level: 3, label: \"Intermediate\", tempo: 100 },\n  { level: 4, label: \"Advanced\", tempo: 120 },\n  { level: 5, label: \"Expert\", tempo: 140 },\n];\n\nconst DAILY_OPTIONS = [5, 10, 15, 20, 30];\n\n// Placeholder seed: single-beat combination cells (one beat = 4 notes),\n// played x4 to fill a 4/4 measure, x20 measures. Ordered easy -> hard.\nconst SEED = [\n  \"RLRL\", \"LRLR\", \"RRLL\", \"LLRR\",\n  \"RLRR\", \"LRLL\", \"RRLR\", \"LLRL\",\n  \"RRRL\", \"LLLR\", \"RLLL\", \"LRRR\",\n  \"RRRR\", \"LLLL\",\n].map((cell, i) => ({\n  id: `sbc-${i + 1}`,\n  name: `Combination ${i + 1}`,\n  cell: cell.split(\"\"),\n}));\n\n// seconds for one exercise = (60/tempo) * beats * repeats\nconst exerciseSeconds = (tempo) =>\n  (60 / tempo) * BEATS_PER_MEASURE * REPEAT_COUNT;\n\nconst fmt = (s) => {\n  const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n  const sec = Math.round(s % 60);\n  return `${m}:${String(sec).padStart(2, \"0\")}`;\n};\n\n// ---- component ------------------------------------------------------------\nexport default function DrumTrainer() {\n  const [screen, setScreen] = useState(\"setup\"); // setup | session | summary\n  const [level, setLevel] = useState(LEVELS[0]);\n  const [dailyMin, setDailyMin] = useState(10);\n\n  // session state\n  const [queue, setQueue] = useState([]);\n  const [goalCount, setGoalCount] = useState(0);\n  const [activeIdx, setActiveIdx] = useState(0);\n  const [completed, setCompleted] = useState([]);\n  const [isPlaying, setIsPlaying] = useState(false);\n  const [noteInMeasure, setNoteInMeasure] = useState(0);\n  const [repsDone, setRepsDone] = useState(0);\n  const [sessionSec, setSessionSec] = useState(0);\n\n  // audio + scheduler refs\n  const ctxRef = useRef(null);\n  const schedTimer = useRef(null);\n  const rafRef = useRef(null);\n  const secTimer = useRef(null);\n  const nextNoteTime = useRef(0);\n  const noteInMeasRef = useRef(0);\n  const measureRef = useRef(0);\n  const idxRef = useRef(0);\n  const queueRef = useRef([]);\n  const noteQ = useRef([]); // scheduled notes for visual sync\n  const tempoRef = useRef(60);\n  const completedRef = useRef([]);\n\n  const tempo = level.tempo;\n  const exSec = exerciseSeconds(tempo);\n\n  // ---- start session ----\n  const startSession = () => {\n    const budget = dailyMin * 60;\n    const fit = Math.max(1, Math.min(SEED.length, Math.floor(budget / exSec)));\n    setQueue(SEED);\n    queueRef.current = SEED;\n    setGoalCount(fit);\n    setActiveIdx(0);\n    setCompleted([]);\n    completedRef.current = [];\n    setSessionSec(0);\n    setRepsDone(0);\n    setNoteInMeasure(0);\n    idxRef.current = 0;\n    measureRef.current = 0;\n    noteInMeasRef.current = 0;\n    noteQ.current = [];\n    tempoRef.current = tempo;\n    setScreen(\"session\");\n  };\n\n  // ---- audio click ----\n  const click = (time, accent) => {\n    const ctx = ctxRef.current;\n    const osc = ctx.createOscillator();\n    const gain = ctx.createGain();\n    osc.frequency.value = accent ? 1400 : 800;\n    gain.gain.setValueAtTime(accent ? 0.5 : 0.22, time);\n    gain.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, time + 0.05);\n    osc.connect(gain).connect(ctx.destination);\n    osc.start(time);\n    osc.stop(time + 0.06);\n  };\n\n  // ---- scheduler ----\n  const scheduler = useCallback(() => {\n    const ctx = ctxRef.current;\n    const secPer16 = 60 / tempoRef.current / NOTES_PER_BEAT;\n    while (nextNoteTime.current < ctx.currentTime + 0.12) {\n      const ex = queueRef.current[idxRef.current];\n      if (!ex) {\n        stopAll(true);\n        return;\n      }\n      const nim = noteInMeasRef.current;\n      click(nextNoteTime.current, nim % NOTES_PER_BEAT === 0);\n      noteQ.current.push({\n        time: nextNoteTime.current,\n        nim,\n        measure: measureRef.current,\n        idx: idxRef.current,\n      });\n      // advance\n      nextNoteTime.current += secPer16;\n      noteInMeasRef.current += 1;\n      if (noteInMeasRef.current >= NOTES_PER_MEASURE) {\n        noteInMeasRef.current = 0;\n        measureRef.current += 1;\n        if (measureRef.current >= REPEAT_COUNT) {\n          // exercise complete\n          const doneIdx = idxRef.current;\n          if (!completedRef.current.includes(doneIdx)) {\n            completedRef.current = [...completedRef.current, doneIdx];\n            setCompleted([...completedRef.current]);\n          }\n          idxRef.current += 1;\n          measureRef.current = 0;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  // ---- visual sync via rAF ----\n  const draw = useCallback(() => {\n    const ctx = ctxRef.current;\n    if (ctx) {\n      while (noteQ.current.length && noteQ.current[0].time <= ctx.currentTime) {\n        const n = noteQ.current.shift();\n        setNoteInMeasure(n.nim);\n        setActiveIdx(n.idx);\n        setRepsDone(n.measure);\n      }\n    }\n    rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(draw);\n  }, []);\n\n  const play = async () => {\n    if (!ctxRef.current) {\n      ctxRef.current = new (window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)();\n    }\n    await ctxRef.current.resume();\n    nextNoteTime.current = ctxRef.current.currentTime + 0.05;\n    setIsPlaying(true);\n    schedTimer.current = setInterval(scheduler, 25);\n    rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(draw);\n    secTimer.current = setInterval(() => setSessionSec((s) => s + 1), 1000);\n  };\n\n  const pause = () => {\n    setIsPlaying(false);\n    clearInterval(schedTimer.current);\n    clearInterval(secTimer.current);\n    cancelAnimationFrame(rafRef.current);\n    noteQ.current = [];\n  };\n\n  const stopAll = (finished) => {\n    pause();\n    if (finished) setScreen(\"summary\");\n  };\n\n  const skip = () => {\n    // mark current as done and jump to next\n    const i = idxRef.current;\n    if (!completedRef.current.includes(i)) {\n      completedRef.current = [...completedRef.current, i];\n      setCompleted([...completedRef.current]);\n    }\n    idxRef.current += 1;\n    measureRef.current = 0;\n    noteInMeasRef.current = 0;\n    noteQ.current = [];\n    if (idxRef.current >= queueRef.current.length) stopAll(true);\n  };\n\n  useEffect(() => () => stopAll(false), []); // cleanup on unmount\n\n  // ---- render: SETUP ----\n  if (screen === \"setup\") {\n    const fit = Math.max(1, Math.floor((dailyMin * 60) / exerciseSeconds(level.tempo)));\n    return (\n      <div className=\"min-h-screen bg-[#14110F] text-[#EDE6DD] flex justify-center font-sans\">\n        <div className=\"w-full max-w-[420px] px-5 py-8 flex flex-col\">\n          <div className=\"mb-8\">\n            <div className=\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase mb-1\">Stick Control</div>\n            <h1 className=\"text-3xl font-black tracking-tight leading-none\">Daily Practice</h1>\n          </div>\n\n          <Section label=\"Start level \u2014 sets your tempo\">\n            <div className=\"grid grid-cols-1 gap-2\">\n              {LEVELS.map((l) => (\n                <button\n                  key={l.level}\n                  onClick={() => setLevel(l)}\n                  className={`flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 rounded-lg border text-left transition\n                    ${level.level === l.level\n                      ? \"border-[#E8A33D] bg-[#241C12]\"\n                      : \"border-[#2E2825] bg-[#1F1A17] hover:border-[#4a423c]\"}`}\n                >\n                  <span className=\"font-semibold\">Level {l.level} \u00b7 {l.label}</span>\n                  <span className=\"font-mono text-[#E8A33D]\">{l.tempo} BPM</span>\n                </button>\n              ))}\n            </div>\n          </Section>\n\n          <Section label=\"Daily goal\">\n            <div className=\"flex gap-2 flex-wrap\">\n              {DAILY_OPTIONS.map((m) => (\n                <button\n                  key={m}\n                  onClick={() => setDailyMin(m)}\n                  className={`flex-1 min-w-[60px] py-3 rounded-lg border font-mono transition\n                    ${dailyMin === m\n                      ? \"border-[#4FB0A5] bg-[#15211F] text-[#4FB0A5]\"\n                      : \"border-[#2E2825] bg-[#1F1A17] text-[#EDE6DD] hover:border-[#4a423c]\"}`}\n                >\n                  {m}m\n                </button>\n              ))}\n            </div>\n          </Section>\n\n          <div className=\"mt-auto pt-6\">\n            <p className=\"text-sm text-[#8A7E73] mb-4 leading-relaxed\">\n              At {level.tempo} BPM, each exercise takes about {Math.round(exerciseSeconds(level.tempo))}s.\n              Your {dailyMin}-minute goal fits about <span className=\"text-[#EDE6DD] font-semibold\">{fit} exercise{fit > 1 ? \"s\" : \"\"}</span>.\n            </p>\n            <button\n              onClick={startSession}\n              className=\"w-full py-4 rounded-xl bg-[#E8A33D] text-[#14110F] font-black text-lg tracking-tight active:scale-[0.99] transition\"\n            >\n              Start practice\n            </button>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    );\n  }\n\n  // ---- render: SUMMARY ----\n  if (screen === \"summary\") {\n    const goalSec = dailyMin * 60;\n    const under = sessionSec < goalSec;\n    return (\n      <div className=\"min-h-screen bg-[#14110F] text-[#EDE6DD] flex justify-center font-sans\">\n        <div className=\"w-full max-w-[420px] px-5 py-10 flex flex-col items-center text-center\">\n          <div className=\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase mb-2\">Session complete</div>\n          <div className={`text-6xl font-black font-mono mb-1 ${under ? \"text-[#E8A33D]\" : \"text-[#4FB0A5]\"}`}>\n            {fmt(sessionSec)}\n          </div>\n          <div className=\"text-[#8A7E73] mb-8\">of your {dailyMin}:00 goal</div>\n\n          <div className=\"w-full rounded-xl border border-[#2E2825] bg-[#1F1A17] p-5 mb-6\">\n            <Row k=\"Exercises completed\" v={`${completed.length} / ${queue.length}`} />\n            <Row k=\"Level\" v={`${level.level} \u00b7 ${level.tempo} BPM`} />\n          </div>\n\n          <div className={`w-full rounded-xl p-4 mb-8 text-sm leading-relaxed border\n            ${under ? \"border-[#5a4321] bg-[#241C12] text-[#E8A33D]\" : \"border-[#234a45] bg-[#15211F] text-[#4FB0A5]\"}`}>\n            {under\n              ? `You trained ${fmt(goalSec - sessionSec)} short of today's goal. Try one more exercise tomorrow to stay on track.`\n              : `Goal met \u2014 nice work. You can keep building speed at the next level when this feels easy.`}\n          </div>\n\n          <button\n            onClick={() => setScreen(\"setup\")}\n            className=\"w-full py-4 rounded-xl bg-[#E8A33D] text-[#14110F] font-black text-lg active:scale-[0.99] transition\"\n          >\n            Done\n          </button>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    );\n  }\n\n  // ---- render: SESSION ----\n  const ex = queue[activeIdx] || queue[0];\n  const cell = ex.cell;\n  const activeInCell = noteInMeasure % cell.length;\n  const activeBeat = Math.floor(noteInMeasure / NOTES_PER_BEAT);\n  const goalReached = completed.length >= goalCount;\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"min-h-screen bg-[#14110F] text-[#EDE6DD] flex justify-center font-sans select-none\">\n      <div className=\"w-full max-w-[420px] px-5 py-6 flex flex-col\">\n        {/* top bar */}\n        <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between mb-1\">\n          <div className=\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase\">\n            Exercise {activeIdx + 1} / {queue.length}\n          </div>\n          <div className=\"font-mono text-sm text-[#8A7E73]\">{fmt(sessionSec)} / {dailyMin}:00</div>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* goal progress bar */}\n        <div className=\"h-1 rounded-full bg-[#2E2825] mb-6 overflow-hidden\">\n          <div\n            className={`h-full transition-all ${goalReached ? \"bg-[#4FB0A5]\" : \"bg-[#E8A33D]\"}`}\n            style={{ width: `${Math.min(100, (sessionSec / (dailyMin * 60)) * 100)}%` }}\n          />\n        </div>\n\n        {/* beat pips */}\n        <div className=\"flex justify-center gap-3 mb-8\">\n          {Array.from({ length: BEATS_PER_MEASURE }).map((_, b) => (\n            <span\n              key={b}\n              className={`w-3 h-3 rounded-full transition-all duration-75\n                ${isPlaying && b === activeBeat ? \"bg-[#EDE6DD] scale-125\" : \"bg-[#2E2825]\"}`}\n            />\n          ))}\n        </div>\n\n        {/* big sticking display */}\n        <div className=\"flex-1 flex flex-col items-center justify-center\">\n          <div className=\"text-xs tracking-[0.25em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase mb-5\">{ex.name}</div>\n          <div className=\"flex gap-3 mb-10\">\n            {cell.map((h, i) => {\n              const isActive = isPlaying && i === activeInCell;\n              const isR = h === \"R\";\n              const base = isR ? \"#E8A33D\" : \"#4FB0A5\";\n              return (\n                <div\n                  key={i}\n                  className=\"w-16 h-20 rounded-xl flex items-center justify-center text-4xl font-black transition-all duration-75\"\n                  style={{\n                    color: isActive ? \"#14110F\" : base,\n                    background: isActive ? base : \"#1F1A17\",\n                    border: `1px solid ${isActive ? base : \"#2E2825\"}`,\n                    transform: isActive ? \"translateY(-4px)\" : \"none\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  {h}\n                </div>\n              );\n            })}\n          </div>\n\n          {/* rep counter */}\n          <div className=\"text-center\">\n            <div className=\"font-mono text-2xl font-bold\">\n              {Math.min(repsDone + (isPlaying ? 1 : 0), REPEAT_COUNT)}\n              <span className=\"text-[#8A7E73]\"> / {REPEAT_COUNT}</span>\n            </div>\n            <div className=\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.25em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase mt-1\">repetitions</div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* legend */}\n        <div className=\"flex justify-center gap-6 mb-5 text-xs\">\n          <span className=\"flex items-center gap-2\"><span className=\"w-3 h-3 rounded bg-[#E8A33D]\" /> Right</span>\n          <span className=\"flex items-center gap-2\"><span className=\"w-3 h-3 rounded bg-[#4FB0A5]\" /> Left</span>\n        </div>\n\n        {goalReached && (\n          <div className=\"text-center text-[#4FB0A5] text-sm mb-4\">Daily goal reached \u2014 keep going or finish up.</div>\n        )}\n\n        {/* transport */}\n        <div className=\"flex gap-3\">\n          <button\n            onClick={isPlaying ? pause : play}\n            className=\"flex-1 py-4 rounded-xl bg-[#E8A33D] text-[#14110F] font-black text-lg active:scale-[0.99] transition\"\n          >\n            {isPlaying ? \"Pause\" : repsDone > 0 ? \"Resume\" : \"Play\"}\n          </button>\n          <button\n            onClick={skip}\n            className=\"px-5 rounded-xl border border-[#2E2825] bg-[#1F1A17] font-semibold text-[#EDE6DD] active:scale-[0.99] transition\"\n          >\n            Done\n          </button>\n          <button\n            onClick={() => stopAll(true)}\n            className=\"px-5 rounded-xl border border-[#2E2825] bg-[#1F1A17] font-semibold text-[#8A7E73] active:scale-[0.99] transition\"\n          >\n            End\n          </button>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\n// ---- small helpers ----\nfunction Section({ label, children }) {\n  return (\n    <div className=\"mb-6\">\n      <div className=\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.25em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase mb-3\">{label}</div>\n      {children}\n    </div>\n  );\n}\nfunction Row({ k, v }) {\n  return (\n    <div className=\"flex justify-between py-1.5 text-sm\">\n      <span className=\"text-[#8A7E73]\">{k}</span>\n      <span className=\"font-mono\">{v}</span>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n"

    "message": "Mobile-first React prototype of the drum rudiment trainer core training loop",
    "integration_name": null,

    "icon_name": "file",

    "display_content": {
      "type": "json_block",
      "json_block": "{\"language\": \"javascript\", \"code\": \"import React, { useState, useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from \\\"react\\\";\\n\\n/**\\n * DrumTrainer \\u2014 core training-session prototype.\\n *\\n * NOTE ON DATA: the exercises below are GENERATED placeholder seed data\\n * (the standard single-beat combinations). In the real app this comes from\\n * your admin panel, where you transcribe exercises from your own book.\\n *\\n * Prototype uses Tailwind (the artifact renderer's styling). The production\\n * app will use SCSS modules per your stack \\u2014 the structure maps 1:1.\\n */\\n\\n// ---- config ---------------------------------------------------------------\\nconst REPEAT_COUNT = 20; // \\\"Repeat each exercise 20 times\\\"\\nconst BEATS_PER_MEASURE = 4;\\nconst NOTES_PER_BEAT = 4; // sixteenth notes\\nconst NOTES_PER_MEASURE = BEATS_PER_MEASURE * NOTES_PER_BEAT; // 16\\n\\nconst LEVELS = [\\n  { level: 1, label: \\\"Beginner\\\", tempo: 60 },\\n  { level: 2, label: \\\"Developing\\\", tempo: 80 },\\n  { level: 3, label: \\\"Intermediate\\\", tempo: 100 },\\n  { level: 4, label: \\\"Advanced\\\", tempo: 120 },\\n  { level: 5, label: \\\"Expert\\\", tempo: 140 },\\n];\\n\\nconst DAILY_OPTIONS = [5, 10, 15, 20, 30];\\n\\n// Placeholder seed: single-beat combination cells (one beat = 4 notes),\\n// played x4 to fill a 4/4 measure, x20 measures. Ordered easy -> hard.\\nconst SEED = [\\n  \\\"RLRL\\\", \\\"LRLR\\\", \\\"RRLL\\\", \\\"LLRR\\\",\\n  \\\"RLRR\\\", \\\"LRLL\\\", \\\"RRLR\\\", \\\"LLRL\\\",\\n  \\\"RRRL\\\", \\\"LLLR\\\", \\\"RLLL\\\", \\\"LRRR\\\",\\n  \\\"RRRR\\\", \\\"LLLL\\\",\\n].map((cell, i) => ({\\n  id: `sbc-${i + 1}`,\\n  name: `Combination ${i + 1}`,\\n  cell: cell.split(\\\"\\\"),\\n}));\\n\\n// seconds for one exercise = (60/tempo) * beats * repeats\\nconst exerciseSeconds = (tempo) =>\\n  (60 / tempo) * BEATS_PER_MEASURE * REPEAT_COUNT;\\n\\nconst fmt = (s) => {\\n  const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\\n  const sec = Math.round(s % 60);\\n  return `${m}:${String(sec).padStart(2, \\\"0\\\")}`;\\n};\\n\\n// ---- component ------------------------------------------------------------\\nexport default function DrumTrainer() {\\n  const [screen, setScreen] = useState(\\\"setup\\\"); // setup | session | summary\\n  const [level, setLevel] = useState(LEVELS[0]);\\n  const [dailyMin, setDailyMin] = useState(10);\\n\\n  // session state\\n  const [queue, setQueue] = useState([]);\\n  const [goalCount, setGoalCount] = useState(0);\\n  const [activeIdx, setActiveIdx] = useState(0);\\n  const [completed, setCompleted] = useState([]);\\n  const [isPlaying, setIsPlaying] = useState(false);\\n  const [noteInMeasure, setNoteInMeasure] = useState(0);\\n  const [repsDone, setRepsDone] = useState(0);\\n  const [sessionSec, setSessionSec] = useState(0);\\n\\n  // audio + scheduler refs\\n  const ctxRef = useRef(null);\\n  const schedTimer = useRef(null);\\n  const rafRef = useRef(null);\\n  const secTimer = useRef(null);\\n  const nextNoteTime = useRef(0);\\n  const noteInMeasRef = useRef(0);\\n  const measureRef = useRef(0);\\n  const idxRef = useRef(0);\\n  const queueRef = useRef([]);\\n  const noteQ = useRef([]); // scheduled notes for visual sync\\n  const tempoRef = useRef(60);\\n  const completedRef = useRef([]);\\n\\n  const tempo = level.tempo;\\n  const exSec = exerciseSeconds(tempo);\\n\\n  // ---- start session ----\\n  const startSession = () => {\\n    const budget = dailyMin * 60;\\n    const fit = Math.max(1, Math.min(SEED.length, Math.floor(budget / exSec)));\\n    setQueue(SEED);\\n    queueRef.current = SEED;\\n    setGoalCount(fit);\\n    setActiveIdx(0);\\n    setCompleted([]);\\n    completedRef.current = [];\\n    setSessionSec(0);\\n    setRepsDone(0);\\n    setNoteInMeasure(0);\\n    idxRef.current = 0;\\n    measureRef.current = 0;\\n    noteInMeasRef.current = 0;\\n    noteQ.current = [];\\n    tempoRef.current = tempo;\\n    setScreen(\\\"session\\\");\\n  };\\n\\n  // ---- audio click ----\\n  const click = (time, accent) => {\\n    const ctx = ctxRef.current;\\n    const osc = ctx.createOscillator();\\n    const gain = ctx.createGain();\\n    osc.frequency.value = accent ? 1400 : 800;\\n    gain.gain.setValueAtTime(accent ? 0.5 : 0.22, time);\\n    gain.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.0001, time + 0.05);\\n    osc.connect(gain).connect(ctx.destination);\\n    osc.start(time);\\n    osc.stop(time + 0.06);\\n  };\\n\\n  // ---- scheduler ----\\n  const scheduler = useCallback(() => {\\n    const ctx = ctxRef.current;\\n    const secPer16 = 60 / tempoRef.current / NOTES_PER_BEAT;\\n    while (nextNoteTime.current < ctx.currentTime + 0.12) {\\n      const ex = queueRef.current[idxRef.current];\\n      if (!ex) {\\n        stopAll(true);\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      const nim = noteInMeasRef.current;\\n      click(nextNoteTime.current, nim % NOTES_PER_BEAT === 0);\\n      noteQ.current.push({\\n        time: nextNoteTime.current,\\n        nim,\\n        measure: measureRef.current,\\n        idx: idxRef.current,\\n      });\\n      // advance\\n      nextNoteTime.current += secPer16;\\n      noteInMeasRef.current += 1;\\n      if (noteInMeasRef.current >= NOTES_PER_MEASURE) {\\n        noteInMeasRef.current = 0;\\n        measureRef.current += 1;\\n        if (measureRef.current >= REPEAT_COUNT) {\\n          // exercise complete\\n          const doneIdx = idxRef.current;\\n          if (!completedRef.current.includes(doneIdx)) {\\n            completedRef.current = [...completedRef.current, doneIdx];\\n            setCompleted([...completedRef.current]);\\n          }\\n          idxRef.current += 1;\\n          measureRef.current = 0;\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }, []);\\n\\n  // ---- visual sync via rAF ----\\n  const draw = useCallback(() => {\\n    const ctx = ctxRef.current;\\n    if (ctx) {\\n      while (noteQ.current.length && noteQ.current[0].time <= ctx.currentTime) {\\n        const n = noteQ.current.shift();\\n        setNoteInMeasure(n.nim);\\n        setActiveIdx(n.idx);\\n        setRepsDone(n.measure);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(draw);\\n  }, []);\\n\\n  const play = async () => {\\n    if (!ctxRef.current) {\\n      ctxRef.current = new (window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)();\\n    }\\n    await ctxRef.current.resume();\\n    nextNoteTime.current = ctxRef.current.currentTime + 0.05;\\n    setIsPlaying(true);\\n    schedTimer.current = setInterval(scheduler, 25);\\n    rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(draw);\\n    secTimer.current = setInterval(() => setSessionSec((s) => s + 1), 1000);\\n  };\\n\\n  const pause = () => {\\n    setIsPlaying(false);\\n    clearInterval(schedTimer.current);\\n    clearInterval(secTimer.current);\\n    cancelAnimationFrame(rafRef.current);\\n    noteQ.current = [];\\n  };\\n\\n  const stopAll = (finished) => {\\n    pause();\\n    if (finished) setScreen(\\\"summary\\\");\\n  };\\n\\n  const skip = () => {\\n    // mark current as done and jump to next\\n    const i = idxRef.current;\\n    if (!completedRef.current.includes(i)) {\\n      completedRef.current = [...completedRef.current, i];\\n      setCompleted([...completedRef.current]);\\n    }\\n    idxRef.current += 1;\\n    measureRef.current = 0;\\n    noteInMeasRef.current = 0;\\n    noteQ.current = [];\\n    if (idxRef.current >= queueRef.current.length) stopAll(true);\\n  };\\n\\n  useEffect(() => () => stopAll(false), []); // cleanup on unmount\\n\\n  // ---- render: SETUP ----\\n  if (screen === \\\"setup\\\") {\\n    const fit = Math.max(1, Math.floor((dailyMin * 60) / exerciseSeconds(level.tempo)));\\n    return (\\n      <div className=\\\"min-h-screen bg-[#14110F] text-[#EDE6DD] flex justify-center font-sans\\\">\\n        <div className=\\\"w-full max-w-[420px] px-5 py-8 flex flex-col\\\">\\n          <div className=\\\"mb-8\\\">\\n            <div className=\\\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase mb-1\\\">Stick Control</div>\\n            <h1 className=\\\"text-3xl font-black tracking-tight leading-none\\\">Daily Practice</h1>\\n          </div>\\n\\n          <Section label=\\\"Start level \\u2014 sets your tempo\\\">\\n            <div className=\\\"grid grid-cols-1 gap-2\\\">\\n              {LEVELS.map((l) => (\\n                <button\\n                  key={l.level}\\n                  onClick={() => setLevel(l)}\\n                  className={`flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 rounded-lg border text-left transition\\n                    ${level.level === l.level\\n                      ? \\\"border-[#E8A33D] bg-[#241C12]\\\"\\n                      : \\\"border-[#2E2825] bg-[#1F1A17] hover:border-[#4a423c]\\\"}`}\\n                >\\n                  <span className=\\\"font-semibold\\\">Level {l.level} \\u00b7 {l.label}</span>\\n                  <span className=\\\"font-mono text-[#E8A33D]\\\">{l.tempo} BPM</span>\\n                </button>\\n              ))}\\n            </div>\\n          </Section>\\n\\n          <Section label=\\\"Daily goal\\\">\\n            <div className=\\\"flex gap-2 flex-wrap\\\">\\n              {DAILY_OPTIONS.map((m) => (\\n                <button\\n                  key={m}\\n                  onClick={() => setDailyMin(m)}\\n                  className={`flex-1 min-w-[60px] py-3 rounded-lg border font-mono transition\\n                    ${dailyMin === m\\n                      ? \\\"border-[#4FB0A5] bg-[#15211F] text-[#4FB0A5]\\\"\\n                      : \\\"border-[#2E2825] bg-[#1F1A17] text-[#EDE6DD] hover:border-[#4a423c]\\\"}`}\\n                >\\n                  {m}m\\n                </button>\\n              ))}\\n            </div>\\n          </Section>\\n\\n          <div className=\\\"mt-auto pt-6\\\">\\n            <p className=\\\"text-sm text-[#8A7E73] mb-4 leading-relaxed\\\">\\n              At {level.tempo} BPM, each exercise takes about {Math.round(exerciseSeconds(level.tempo))}s.\\n              Your {dailyMin}-minute goal fits about <span className=\\\"text-[#EDE6DD] font-semibold\\\">{fit} exercise{fit > 1 ? \\\"s\\\" : \\\"\\\"}</span>.\\n            </p>\\n            <button\\n              onClick={startSession}\\n              className=\\\"w-full py-4 rounded-xl bg-[#E8A33D] text-[#14110F] font-black text-lg tracking-tight active:scale-[0.99] transition\\\"\\n            >\\n              Start practice\\n            </button>\\n          </div>\\n        </div>\\n      </div>\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  // ---- render: SUMMARY ----\\n  if (screen === \\\"summary\\\") {\\n    const goalSec = dailyMin * 60;\\n    const under = sessionSec < goalSec;\\n    return (\\n      <div className=\\\"min-h-screen bg-[#14110F] text-[#EDE6DD] flex justify-center font-sans\\\">\\n        <div className=\\\"w-full max-w-[420px] px-5 py-10 flex flex-col items-center text-center\\\">\\n          <div className=\\\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase mb-2\\\">Session complete</div>\\n          <div className={`text-6xl font-black font-mono mb-1 ${under ? \\\"text-[#E8A33D]\\\" : \\\"text-[#4FB0A5]\\\"}`}>\\n            {fmt(sessionSec)}\\n          </div>\\n          <div className=\\\"text-[#8A7E73] mb-8\\\">of your {dailyMin}:00 goal</div>\\n\\n          <div className=\\\"w-full rounded-xl border border-[#2E2825] bg-[#1F1A17] p-5 mb-6\\\">\\n            <Row k=\\\"Exercises completed\\\" v={`${completed.length} / ${queue.length}`} />\\n            <Row k=\\\"Level\\\" v={`${level.level} \\u00b7 ${level.tempo} BPM`} />\\n          </div>\\n\\n          <div className={`w-full rounded-xl p-4 mb-8 text-sm leading-relaxed border\\n            ${under ? \\\"border-[#5a4321] bg-[#241C12] text-[#E8A33D]\\\" : \\\"border-[#234a45] bg-[#15211F] text-[#4FB0A5]\\\"}`}>\\n            {under\\n              ? `You trained ${fmt(goalSec - sessionSec)} short of today's goal. Try one more exercise tomorrow to stay on track.`\\n              : `Goal met \\u2014 nice work. You can keep building speed at the next level when this feels easy.`}\\n          </div>\\n\\n          <button\\n            onClick={() => setScreen(\\\"setup\\\")}\\n            className=\\\"w-full py-4 rounded-xl bg-[#E8A33D] text-[#14110F] font-black text-lg active:scale-[0.99] transition\\\"\\n          >\\n            Done\\n          </button>\\n        </div>\\n      </div>\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  // ---- render: SESSION ----\\n  const ex = queue[activeIdx] || queue[0];\\n  const cell = ex.cell;\\n  const activeInCell = noteInMeasure % cell.length;\\n  const activeBeat = Math.floor(noteInMeasure / NOTES_PER_BEAT);\\n  const goalReached = completed.length >= goalCount;\\n\\n  return (\\n    <div className=\\\"min-h-screen bg-[#14110F] text-[#EDE6DD] flex justify-center font-sans select-none\\\">\\n      <div className=\\\"w-full max-w-[420px] px-5 py-6 flex flex-col\\\">\\n        {/* top bar */}\\n        <div className=\\\"flex items-center justify-between mb-1\\\">\\n          <div className=\\\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] text-[#8A7E73] uppercase\\\">\\n            Exercise {activeIdx + 1} / {queue.length}\\n          </div>\\n          <div className=\\\"font-mono text-sm text-[#8A7E73]\\\">{fmt(sessionSec)} / {dailyMin}:00</div>\\n        </div>\\n\\n        {/* goal progress bar */}\\n        <div className=\\\"h-1 rounded-full bg-[#2E2825] mb-6 overflow-hidden\\\">\\n          <div\\n            className={`h-full transition-all ${goalReached ? \\\"bg-[#4FB0A5]\\\" : \\\"bg-[#E8A33D]\\\"}`}\\n            style={{ width: `${Math.min(100, (sessionSec / (dailyMin * 60)) * 100)}%` }}\\n          />\\n        </div>\\n\\n        {/* beat pips */}\\n        <div className=\\\"flex justify-center gap-3 mb-8\\\">\\n          {Array.from({ length: BEATS_PER_MEASURE }).map((_, b) => (\\n            <span\\n              key={b}\\n              className={`w-3 h-3 rounded-full transition-all duration-75\\n                ${isPlaying && b === activeBeat ? A 10-min goal fits ~7 exercises \u2014 matching your example. Raising the level raises tempo, which *shortens* each exercise, so more fit per day.       "file_text": "import React, { useState, useRef, useMemo } from \"react\";\n\n/**\n * ExerciseAdmin \u2014 author exercises from a book page you own.\n *\n * Workflow: upload the page scan (e.g. Stick Control p.5) -> it stays\n * pinned beside the form -> tap R/L to transcribe each line's sticking ->\n * add it to the list -> reorder to match the book's buildup -> export JSON.\n *\n * The exported JSON is exactly the shape the trainer/metronome screen reads:\n *   { id, name, source, pattern:[...], notesPerBeat, repeatCount, difficulty, order }\n *\n * Prototype uses Tailwind + in-memory state (no localStorage in artifacts).\n * Use Export/Import to persist your work. Production = SCSS + MERN API.\n */\n\nconst C = {\n  bg: \"#14110F\", panel: \"#1F1A17\", border: \"#2E2825\",\n  text: \"#EDE6DD\", muted: \"#8A7E73\", R: \"#E8A33D\", L: \"#4FB0A5\",\n};\n\nconst emptyForm = {\n  source: \"Single Beat Combinations \u00b7 p.5\",\n  name: \"\",\n  pattern: [],\n  notesPerBeat: 4,\n  repeatCount: 20,\n  difficulty: 1,\n};\n\nexport default function ExerciseAdmin() {\n  const [image, setImage] = useState(null);\n  const [exercises, setExercises] = useState([]);\n  const [form, setForm] = useState(emptyForm);\n  const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState(null);\n  const [importText, setImportText] = useState(\"\");\n  const [status, setStatus] = useState(\"\");\n  const jsonRef = useRef(null);\n\n  const setField = (k, v) => setForm((f) => ({ ...f, [k]: v }));\n\n  const beatsPerMeasure = form.pattern.length / form.notesPerBeat;\n  const durAt60 = beatsPerMeasure ? Math.round((60 / 60) * beatsPerMeasure * form.repeatCount) : 0;\n\n  const json = useMemo(\n    () => JSON.stringify(exercises, null, 2),\n    [exercises]\n  );\n\n  // ---- pattern builder ----\n  const appendNote = (h) => setField(\"pattern\", [...form.pattern, h]);\n  const backspace = () => setField(\"pattern\", form.pattern.slice(0, -1));\n  const clearPattern = () => setField(\"pattern\", []);\n  const fillMeasure = () => {\n    const target = form.notesPerBeat * 4;\n    if (!form.pattern.length || form.pattern.length >= target) return;\n    const out = [];\n    let i = 0;\n    while (out.length < target) out.push(form.pattern[i++ % form.pattern.length]);\n    setField(\"pattern\", out);\n  };\n\n  // ---- list ops ----\n  const addOrUpdate = () => {\n    if (!form.pattern.length) { setStatus(\"Add at least one sticking note.\"); return; }\n    if (editingId) {\n      setExercises((xs) => xs.map((x) => (x.id === editingId ? { ...x, ...form, name: form.name || x.name } : x)));\n      setEditingId(null);\n    } else {\n      const order = exercises.length + 1;\n      setExercises((xs) => [\n        ...xs,\n        {\n          id: `ex-${Date.now()}`,\n          ...form,\n          name: form.name || `Exercise ${order}`,\n          order,\n          sourceImageUrl: image ? \"(uploaded reference)\" : null,\n        },\n      ]);\n    }\n    setForm((f) => ({ ...emptyForm, source: f.source, notesPerBeat: f.notesPerBeat, repeatCount: f.repeatCount }));\n    setStatus(\"\");\n  };\n\n  const edit = (x) => { setForm({ source: x.source, name: x.name, pattern: x.pattern, notesPerBeat: x.notesPerBeat, repeatCount: x.repeatCount, difficulty: x.difficulty }); setEditingId(x.id); };\n  const del = (id) => { setExercises((xs) => xs.filter((x) => x.id !== id).map((x, i) => ({ ...x, order: i + 1 }))); if (editingId === id) { setEditingId(null); setForm(emptyForm); } };\n  const move = (id, dir) => {\n    setExercises((xs) => {\n      const i = xs.findIndex((x) => x.id === id);\n      const j = i + dir;\n      if (j < 0 || j >= xs.length) return xs;\n      const next = [...xs];\n      [next[i], next[j]] = [next[j], next[i]];\n      return next.map((x, k) => ({ ...x, order: k + 1 }));\n    });\n  };\n\n  const copyJson = async () => {\n    try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(json); setStatus(\"JSON copied.\"); }\n    catch { jsonRef.current?.select(); document.execCommand?.(\"copy\"); setStatus(\"Selected \u2014 press Cmd/Ctrl+C.\"); }\n  };\n  const doImport = () => {\n    try {\n      const data = JSON.parse(importText);\n      if (!Array.isArray(data)) throw new Error();\n      setExercises(data.map((x, i) => ({ ...x, order: i + 1 })));\n      setImportText(\"\"); setStatus(`Imported ${data.length} exercises.`);\n    } catch { setStatus(\"Couldn't parse that JSON.\"); }\n  };\n\n  const onFile = (e) => {\n    const file = e.target.files?.[0];\n    if (!file) return;\n    const r = new FileReader();\n    r.onload = () => setImage(r.result);\n    r.readAsDataURL(file);\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"min-h-screen font-sans\" style={{ background: C.bg, color: C.text }}>\n      <div className=\"max-w-[1100px] mx-auto px-4 py-6\">\n        {/* header */}\n        <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between mb-6\">\n          <div>\n            <div className=\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] uppercase mb-1\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>Stick Control \u00b7 Admin</div>\n            <h1 className=\"text-2xl font-black tracking-tight\">Exercise authoring</h1>\n          </div>\n          <div className=\"text-sm font-mono\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>{exercises.length} saved</div>\n        </div>\n\n        <div className=\"grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-5\">\n          {/* LEFT: reference image */}\n          <div>\n            <Label>Reference page</Label>\n            <div className=\"rounded-xl border overflow-hidden md:sticky md:top-4\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, background: C.panel }}>\n              {image ? (\n                <img src={image} alt=\"reference\" className=\"w-full block\" />\n              ) : (\n                <label className=\"flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-64 cursor-pointer text-center px-6\">\n                  <div className=\"text-4xl mb-2\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>+</div>\n                  <div className=\"font-semibold mb-1\">Upload your page scan</div>\n                  <div className=\"text-sm\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>It stays pinned here while you transcribe.</div>\n                  <input type=\"file\" accept=\"image/*\" onChange={onFile} className=\"hidden\" />\n                </label>\n              )}\n              {image && (\n                <label className=\"block text-center py-2 text-sm cursor-pointer border-t\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, color: C.muted }}>\n                  Replace image\n                  <input type=\"file\" accept=\"image/*\" onChange={onFile} className=\"hidden\" />\n                </label>\n              )}\n            </div>\n          </div>\n\n          {/* RIGHT: form */}\n          <div>\n            <Label>{editingId ? \"Edit exercise\" : \"New exercise\"}</Label>\n            <div className=\"rounded-xl border p-4\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, background: C.panel }}>\n              <Field label=\"Source / page\">\n                <input value={form.source} onChange={(e) => setField(\"source\", e.target.value)}\n                  className=\"w-full rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm outline-none\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}`, color: C.text }} />\n              </Field>\n              <Field label=\"Name (optional)\">\n                <input value={form.name} placeholder={`Exercise ${exercises.length + 1}`} onChange={(e) => setField(\"name\", e.target.value)}\n                  className=\"w-full rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm outline-none placeholder:text-[#5a524b]\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}`, color: C.text }} />\n              </Field>\n\n              {/* sticking builder */}\n              <Field label=\"Sticking \u2014 tap to build the written measure\">\n                <div className=\"rounded-lg p-3 mb-3 min-h-[56px] flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 items-center\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}` }}>\n                  {form.pattern.length === 0 && <span className=\"text-sm\" style={{ color: \"#5a524b\" }}>Tap R / L below\u2026</span>}\n                  {form.pattern.map((h, i) => (\n                    <React.Fragment key={i}>\n                      {i > 0 && i % form.notesPerBeat === 0 && <span className=\"w-px h-6 mx-0.5\" style={{ background: C.border }} />}\n                      <span className=\"w-7 h-7 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-sm font-black\"\n                        style={{ color: C.bg, background: h === \"R\" ? C.R : C.L }}>{h}</span>\n                    </React.Fragment>\n                  ))}\n                </div>\n                <div className=\"grid grid-cols-4 gap-2\">\n                  <button onClick={() => appendNote(\"R\")} className=\"py-3 rounded-lg font-black text-lg active:scale-95 transition\" style={{ background: C.R, color: C.bg }}>R</button>\n                  <button onClick={() => appendNote(\"L\")} className=\"py-3 rounded-lg font-black text-lg active:scale-95 transition\" style={{ background: C.L, color: C.bg }}>L</button>\n                  <button onClick={backspace} className=\"py-3 rounded-lg font-semibold active:scale-95 transition\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}`, color: C.text }}>\u232b</button>\n                  <button onClick={clearPattern} className=\"py-3 rounded-lg font-semibold active:scale-95 transition\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}`, color: C.muted }}>Clear</button>\n                </div>\n                <button onClick={fillMeasure} className=\"mt-2 w-full py-2 rounded-lg text-sm active:scale-95 transition\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}`, color: C.muted }}>\n                  Fill to 4 beats (repeat current)\n                </button>\n              </Field>\n\n              {/* numeric settings */}\n              <div className=\"grid          </select>\n                </Field>\n              </div>\n\n              {beatsPerMeasure > 0 && (\n                <div className=\"text-xs mb-3\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>\n                  {beatsPerMeasure % 1 === 0 ? beatsPerMeasure : beatsPerMeasure.toFixed(2)} beat{beatsPerMeasure !== 1 ? \"s\" : \"\"}/measure \u00b7 ~{durAt60}s per exercise at 60 BPM\n                </div>\n              )}\n\n              <div className=\"flex gap-2\">\n                <button onClick={addOrUpdate} className=\"flex-1 py-3 rounded-lg font-black active:scale-[0.99] transition\" style={{ {/* LIST */}\n        <div className=\"mt-8\">\n          <Label>Exercises \u2014 in practice order</Label>\n          {exercises.length === 0 ? (\n            <div className=\"rounded-xl border p-8 text-center text-sm\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, color: C.muted }}>\n              No exercises yet. Transcribe the first line from your page above.\n            </div>\n          ) : (\n            <div className=\"space-y-2\">\n              {exercises.map((x, i) => (\n                <div key={x.id} className=\"rounded-xl border p-3 flex items-center gap-3\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, background: C.panel }}>\n                  <div className=\"font-mono text-sm w-6 text-center\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>{x.order}</div>\n                  <div className=\"flex-1 min-w-0\">\n                    <div className=\"font-semibold text-sm truncate\">{x.name}</div>\n                    <div className=\"flex flex-wrap gap-1 mt-1\">\n                      {x.pattern.map((h, k) => (\n                        <span key={k} className=\"w-5 h-5 rounded text-[11px] font-black flex items-center justify-center\" style={{ color: C.bg, background: h === \"R\" ? </div>\n  );\n}\nfunction IconBtn({ children, onClick, disabled, danger }) {\n  return (\n    <button onClick={onClick} disabled={disabled} className=\"w-8 h-8 rounded-lg text-sm active:scale-90       "json_block": "{\"language\": \"javascript\", \"code\": \"import React, { useState, useRef, useMemo } from \\\"react\\\";\\n\\n/**\\n * ExerciseAdmin \\u2014 author exercises from a book page you own.\\n *\\n * Workflow: upload the page scan (e.g. Stick Control p.5) -> it stays\\n * pinned beside the form -> tap R/L to transcribe each line's sticking ->\\n * add it to the list -> reorder to match the book's buildup -> export JSON.\\n *\\n * The exported JSON is exactly the shape the trainer/metronome screen reads:\\n *   { id, name, source, pattern:[...], notesPerBeat, repeatCount, difficulty, order }\\n *\\n * Prototype uses Tailwind + in-memory state (no localStorage in artifacts).\\n * Use Export/Import to persist your work. Production = SCSS + MERN API.\\n */\\n\\nconst C = {\\n  bg: \\\"#14110F\\\", panel: \\\"#1F1A17\\\", border: \\\"#2E2825\\\",\\n  text: \\\"#EDE6DD\\\", muted: \\\"#8A7E73\\\", R: \\\"#E8A33D\\\", L: \\\"#4FB0A5\\\",\\n};\\n\\nconst emptyForm = {\\n  source: \\\"Single Beat Combinations \\u00b7 p.5\\\",\\n  name: \\\"\\\",\\n  pattern: [],\\n  notesPerBeat: 4,\\n  repeatCount: 20,\\n  difficulty: 1,\\n};\\n\\nexport default function ExerciseAdmin() {\\n  const [image, setImage] = useState(null);\\n  const [exercises, setExercises] = useState([]);\\n  const [form, setForm] = useState(emptyForm);\\n  const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState(null);\\n  const [importText, setImportText] = useState(\\\"\\\");\\n  const [status, setStatus] = useState(\\\"\\\");\\n  const jsonRef = useRef(null);\\n\\n  const setField = (k, v) => setForm((f) => ({ ...f, [k]: v }));\\n\\n  const beatsPerMeasure = form.pattern.length / form.notesPerBeat;\\n  const durAt60 = beatsPerMeasure ? Math.round((60 / 60) * beatsPerMeasure * form.repeatCount) : 0;\\n\\n  const json = useMemo(\\n    () => JSON.stringify(exercises, null, 2),\\n    [exercises]\\n  );\\n\\n  // ---- pattern builder ----\\n  const appendNote = (h) => setField(\\\"pattern\\\", [...form.pattern, h]);\\n  const backspace = () => setField(\\\"pattern\\\", form.pattern.slice(0, -1));\\n  const clearPattern = () => setField(\\\"pattern\\\", []);\\n  const fillMeasure = () => {\\n    const target = form.notesPerBeat * 4;\\n    if (!form.pattern.length || form.pattern.length >= target) return;\\n    const out = [];\\n    let i = 0;\\n    while (out.length < target) out.push(form.pattern[i++ % form.pattern.length]);\\n    setField(\\\"pattern\\\", out);\\n  };\\n\\n  // ---- list ops ----\\n  const addOrUpdate = () => {\\n    if (!form.pattern.length) { setStatus(\\\"Add at least one sticking note.\\\"); return; }\\n    if (editingId) {\\n      setExercises((xs) => xs.map((x) => (x.id === editingId ? { ...x, ...form, name: form.name || x.name } : x)));\\n      setEditingId(null);\\n    } else {\\n      const order = exercises.length + 1;\\n      setExercises((xs) => [\\n        ...xs,\\n        {\\n          id: `ex-${Date.now()}`,\\n          ...form,\\n          name: form.name || `Exercise ${order}`,\\n          order,\\n          sourceImageUrl: image ? \\\"(uploaded reference)\\\" : null,\\n        },\\n      ]);\\n    }\\n    setForm((f) => ({ ...emptyForm, source: f.source, notesPerBeat: f.notesPerBeat, repeatCount: f.repeatCount }));\\n    setStatus(\\\"\\\");\\n  };\\n\\n  const edit = (x) => { setForm({ source: x.source, name: x.name, pattern: x.pattern, notesPerBeat: x.notesPerBeat, repeatCount: x.repeatCount, difficulty: x.difficulty }); setEditingId(x.id); };\\n  const del = (id) => { setExercises((xs) => xs.filter((x) => x.id !== id).map((x, i) => ({ ...x, order: i + 1 }))); if (editingId === id) { setEditingId(null); setForm(emptyForm); } };\\n  const move = (id, dir) => {\\n    setExercises((xs) => {\\n      const i = xs.findIndex((x) => x.id === id);\\n      const j = i + dir;\\n      if (j < 0 || j >= xs.length) return xs;\\n      const next = [...xs];\\n      [next[i], next[j]] = [next[j], next[i]];\\n      return next.map((x, k) => ({ ...x, order: k + 1 }));\\n    });\\n  };\\n\\n  const copyJson = async () => {\\n    try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(json); setStatus(\\\"JSON copied.\\\"); }\\n    catch { jsonRef.current?.select(); document.execCommand?.(\\\"copy\\\"); setStatus(\\\"Selected \\u2014 press Cmd/Ctrl+C.\\\"); }\\n  };\\n  const doImport = () => {\\n    try {\\n      const data = JSON.parse(importText);\\n      if (!Array.isArray(data)) throw new Error();\\n      setExercises(data.map((x, i) => ({ ...x, order: i + 1 })));\\n      setImportText(\\\"\\\"); setStatus(`Imported ${data.length} exercises.`);\\n    } catch { setStatus(\\\"Couldn't parse that JSON.\\\"); }\\n  };\\n\\n  const onFile = (e) => {\\n    const file = e.target.files?.[0];\\n    if (!file) return;\\n    const r = new FileReader();\\n    r.onload = () => setImage(r.result);\\n    r.readAsDataURL(file);\\n  };\\n\\n  return (\\n    <div className=\\\"min-h-screen font-sans\\\" style={{ background: C.bg, color: C.text }}>\\n      <div className=\\\"max-w-[1100px] mx-auto px-4 py-6\\\">\\n        {/* header */}\\n        <div className=\\\"flex items-center justify-between mb-6\\\">\\n          <div>\\n            <div className=\\\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] uppercase mb-1\\\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>Stick Control \\u00b7 Admin</div>\\n            <h1 className=\\\"text-2xl font-black tracking-tight\\\">Exercise authoring</h1>\\n          </div>\\n          <div className=\\\"text-sm font-mono\\\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>{exercises.length} saved</div>\\n        </div>\\n\\n        <div className=\\\"grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-5\\\">\\n          {/* LEFT: reference image */}\\n          <div>\\n            <Label>Reference page</Label>\\n            <div className=\\\"rounded-xl border overflow-hidden md:sticky md:top-4\\\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, background: C.panel }}>\\n              {image ? (\\n                <img src={image} alt=\\\"reference\\\" className=\\\"w-full block\\\" />\\n              ) : (\\n                <label className=\\\"flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-64 cursor-pointer text-center px-6\\\">\\n                  <div className=\\\"text-4xl mb-2\\\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>+</div>\\n                  <div className=\\\"font-semibold mb-1\\\">Upload your page scan</div>\\n                  <div className=\\\"text-sm\\\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>It stays pinned here while you transcribe.</div>\\n                  <input type=\\\"file\\\" accept=\\\"image/*\\\" onChange={onFile} className=\\\"hidden\\\" />\\n                </label>\\n              )}\\n              {image && (\\n                <label className=\\\"block text-center py-2 text-sm cursor-pointer border-t\\\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, color: C.muted }}>\\n                  Replace image\\n                  <input type=\\\"file\\\" accept=\\\"image/*\\\" onChange={onFile} className=\\\"hidden\\\" />\\n                </label>\\n              )}\\n            </div>\\n          </div>\\n\\n          {/* RIGHT: form */}\\n          <div>\\n            <Label>{editingId ? \\\"Edit exercise\\\" : \\\"New exercise\\\"}</Label>\\n            <div className=\\\"rounded-xl border p-4\\\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, background: C.panel }}>\\n              <Field label=\\\"Source / page\\\">\\n                <input value={form.source} onChange={(e) => setField(\\\"source\\\", e.target.value)}\\n                  className=\\\"w-full rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm outline-none\\\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}`, color: C.text }} />\\n              </Field>\\n              <Field label=\\\"Name (optional)\\\">\\n                <input value={form.name} placeholder={`Exercise ${exercises.length + 1}`} onChange={(e) => setField(\\\"name\\\", e.target.value)}\\n                  className=\\\"w-full rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm outline-none placeholder:text-[#5a524b]\\\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}`, color: C.text }} />\\n              </Field>\\n\\n              {/* sticking builder */}\\n              <Field label=\\\"Sticking \\u2014 tap to build the written measure\\\">\\n                <div className=\\\"rounded-lg p-3 mb-3 min-h-[56px] flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 items-center\\\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}` }}>\\n                  {form.pattern.length === 0 && <span className=\\\"text-sm\\\" style={{ color: \\\"#5a524b\\\" }}>Tap R / L below\\u2026</span>}\\n                  {form.pattern.map((h, i) => (\\n                    <React.Fragment key={i}>\\n                      {i > 0 && i % form.notesPerBeat === 0 && <span className=\\\"w-px h-6 mx-0.5\\\" style={{ background: C.border }} />}\\n                      <span className=\\\"w-7 h-7 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-sm font-black\\\"\\n                        style={{ color: C.bg, background: h === \\\"R\\\" ? <button onClick={() => appendNote(\\\"R\\\")} className=\\\"py-3 rounded-lg font-black text-lg active:scale-95 transition\\\" style={{ background: C.R, color: C.bg }}>R</button>\\n                  <button onClick={() => appendNote(\\\"L\\\")} className=\\\"py-3 rounded-lg font-black text-lg active:scale-95 transition\\\" style={{ background: C.L, color: C.bg }}>L</button>\\n                  <button onClick={backspace} className=\\\"py-3 rounded-lg font-semibold active:scale-95 transition\\\" style={{ background: C.bg, border: `1px solid ${C.border}`, color: C.text }}>\\u232b</button>\\n }}>Clear</button>\\n                </div>\\n                <button onClick={fillMeasure} className=\\\"mt-2 w-full py-2 rounded-lg text-sm active:scale-95 transition\\\" style={{ background: C.bg,              </select>\\n                </Field>\\n                <Field label=\\\"Repeats\\\">\\n                  <input type=\\\"number\\\" min={1} value={form.repeatCount} onChange={(e) =>       {[1, 2, 3, 4, 5].map((d) => <option key={d} value={d}>{d}</option>)}\\n                  </select>\\n                </Field>\\n              </div>\\n\\n              {beatsPerMeasure > 0 &&       "description": "Admin panel v2: auto-extract exercises from an uploaded page image using Claude vision, with review/edit, reorder, export",
      "file_text": "import React, { useState, useMemo } from \"react\";\n\n/**\n * ExerciseAdminAI \u2014 drop in a page scan, the app reads it.\n *\n * Uploads the image -> calls the Anthropic API (Claude vision) -> gets back\n * the exercises as structured JSON (name + R/L sticking) -> you review,\n * correct any misreads, reorder, and export.\n *\n * The export shape matches what the trainer/metronome reads:\n *   { id, name, source, pattern:[\"R\",\"L\",...], notesPerBeat, repeatCount, difficulty, order }\n *\n * Prototype: Tailwind + in-memory state. Production: SCSS + MERN, and the\n * extraction call moves server-side so the API stays behind your backend.\n */\n\nconst C = {\n  bg: \"#14110F\", panel: \"#1F1A17\", border: \"#2E2825\",\n  text: \"#EDE6DD\", muted: \"#8A7E73\", R: \"#E8A33D\", L: \"#4FB0A5\",\n};\n\nconst sanitize = (s) => s.toUpperCase().replace(/[^RL]/g, \"\");\n\nexport default function ExerciseAdminAI() {\n  const [imageData, setImageData] = useState(null); // full data URL for preview\n  const [imageB64, setImageB64] = useState(null);    // base64 payload\n  const [mediaType, setMediaType] = useState(\"image/png\");\n  const [source, setSource] = useState(\"Single Beat Combinations \u00b7 p.5\");\n  const [defaults, setDefaults] = useState({ notesPerBeat: 4, repeatCount: 20, difficulty: 1 });\n  const [exercises, setExercises] = useState([]); // {id, name, pattern(string)}\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n  const [status, setStatus] = useState(\"\");\n  const [importText, setImportText] = useState(\"\");\n\n  const onFile = (e) => {\n    const file = e.target.files?.[0];\n    if (!file) return;\n    setMediaType(file.type || \"image/png\");\n    const r = new FileReader();\n    r.onload = () => {\n      setImageData(r.result);\n      setImageB64(String(r.result).split(\",\")[1]);\n      setStatus(\"\");\n    };\n    r.readAsDataURL(file);\n  };\n\n  // ---- the magic: read the page with Claude vision ----\n  const extract = async () => {\n    if (!imageB64) { setStatus(\"Upload a page image first.\"); return; }\n    setLoading(true);\n    setStatus(\"Reading the page\u2026\");\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch(\"https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages\", {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          model: \"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\n          max_tokens: 1000,\n          system:\n            \"You read snare-drum practice pages. Under each numbered exercise are printed sticking letters R (right) and L (left). \" +\n            \"Return ONLY a compact JSON array, no markdown, no commentary. \" +\n            'Each item: {\"name\":string,\"pattern\":string}. ' +\n            '\"pattern\" is the full run of R/L letters printed for that exercise\\'s written measure, uppercase, no spaces (e.g. \"RLRRLRLL\"). ' +\n            \"Include every numbered exercise, in order: top-to-bottom in the left column, then the right column. Output JSON only.\",\n          messages: [\n            {\n              role: \"user\",\n              content: [\n                { type: \"image\", source: { type: \"base64\", media_type: mediaType, data: imageB64 } },\n                { type: \"text\", text: \"Extract all exercises from this page as JSON.\" },\n              ],\n            },\n          ],\n        }),\n      });\n      const data = await res.json();\n      const text = (data.content || [])\n        .filter((b) => b.type === \"text\").map((b) => b.text).join(\"\\n\")\n        .replace(/```json|```/g, \"\").trim();\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(text);\n      if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) throw new Error(\"not an array\");\n      setExercises(\n        parsed.map((p, i) => ({\n          id: `ex-${Date.now()}-${i}`,\n          name: p.name || `Exercise ${i + 1}`,\n          pattern: sanitize(String(p.pattern || \"\")),\n        }))\n      );\n      setStatus(`Read ${parsed.length} exercises. Check them against the page and fix any misreads.`);\n    } catch (err) {\n      setStatus(\"Couldn't read that automatically \u2014 try a sharper/cropped image, or add lines manually below.\");\n    } finally {\n      setLoading(false);\n    }\n  };\n\n  // ---- edit ops ----\n  const update = (id, key, val) => setExercises((xs) => xs.map((x) => (x.id === id ? { ...x, [key]: key === \"pattern\" ? sanitize(val) : val } : x)));\n  const del = (id) => setExercises((xs) => xs.filter((x) => x.id !== id));\n  const move = (id, dir) => setExercises((xs) => {\n    const i = xs.findIndex((x) => x.id === id); const j = i + dir;\n    if (j < 0 || j >= xs.length) return xs;\n    const n = [...xs]; [n[i], n[j]] = [n[j], n[i]]; return n;\n  });\n  const addBlank = () => setExercises((xs) => [...xs, { id: `ex-${Date.now()}`, name: `Exercise ${xs.length + 1}`, pattern: \"\" }]);\n\n  // ---- export shape (matches the trainer) ----\n  const json = useMemo(() => JSON.stringify(\n    exercises.map((x, i) => ({\n      id: x.id, name: x.name, source,\n      pattern: x.pattern.split(\"\"),\n      notesPerBeat: defaults.notesPerBeat,\n      repeatCount: defaults.repeatCount,\n      difficulty: defaults.difficulty,\n      order: i + 1,\n    })), null, 2), [exercises, source, defaults]);\n\n  const copyJson = async () => {\n    try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(json); setStatus(\"JSON copied.\"); }\n    catch { setStatus(\"Copy blocked \u2014 select the text manually.\"); }\n  };\n  const doImport = () => {\n    try {\n      const arr = JSON.parse(importText);\n      setExercises(arr.map((x, i) => ({ id: x.id || `ex-${Date.now()}-${i}`, name: x.name, pattern: Array.isArray(x.pattern) ? x.pattern.join(\"\") : sanitize(x.pattern || \"\") })));\n      setStatus(`Imported ${arr.length} exercises.`); setImportText(\"\");\n    } catch { setStatus(\"Couldn't parse that JSON.\"); }\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"min-h-screen font-sans\" style={{ background: C.bg, color: C.text }}>\n      <div className=\"max-w-[1100px] mx-auto px-4 py-6\">\n        <div className=\"mb-6\">\n          <div className=\"text-[11px] tracking-[0.3em] uppercase mb-1\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>Stick Control \u00b7 Admin</div>\n          <h1 className=\"text-2xl font-black tracking-tight\">Read a page</h1>\n        </div>\n\n        <div className=\"grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-5\">\n          {/* image + extract */}\n          <div>\n            <Label>Page image</Label>\n            <div className=\"rounded-xl border overflow-hidden mb-3\" style={{ borderColor: C.border, background: C.panel }}>\n              {imageData ? <img src={imageData} alt=\"page\" className=\"w-full block\" /> : (\n                <label className=\"flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-56 cursor-pointer text-center px-6\">\n                  <div className=\"text-4xl mb-2\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>+</div>\n                  <div className=\"font-semibold mb-1\">Upload your page scan</div>\n                  <div className=\"text-sm\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>The app reads the stickings for you.</div>\n                  <input type=\"file\" accept=\"image/*\" onChange={onFile} className=\"hidden\" />\n                </label>\n              )}\n            </div>\n            {imageData && (\n              <label className=\"block text-center mb-3 text-sm cursor-pointer\" style={{ color: C.muted }}>\n                Replace image\n                <input type=\"file\" accept=\"image/*\" onChange={onFile} className=\"hidden\" />\n              </label>\n            )}\n            <button onClick={extract} disabled={loading || !imageB64}\n              className=\"w-full py-4 rounded-xl font-black text-lg active:scale-[0.99] transition disabled:opacity-40\"\n              style={{ background: C.R, color: C.bg }}>\n              {loading ? \"Reading\u2026\" : \"Read exercises from image\"}\n
